title: Build the long-duration epistemic-gain harness
description: Build the long-duration epistemic-gain harness. Current repo truth is strongest on substrate durability, survival, and replayability, but the dream version requires proof that more runtime buys more correct, more complete, or more honestly caveated inquiry rather than just longer wall-clock time. Scope: measure epistemic gain, not just runtime survival, on the same case recipes under forced interruption and governance pressure. Exclusions: worked-once anecdotes, answer-length proxies, and silent suppression of lawful regress or unresolveds.
design: Primary refs: canon/specs/dream-version-proof-charter.md; docs/exec-plans/active/dream_version_proof_wave.md; existing cxdb/replay harnesses as bounded substrate only. Implementation slice: same-case short-vs-long comparisons, forced compaction/checkpoint/restore at adversarial points, steering and contradiction injection during long runs, and explicit gain metrics for supported claims, unresolveds, claim posture, reopened threads, lawful regress, and terminal status.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The harness compares short-vs-long runs on the same recipe rather than across different tasks.
2. Adversarial compaction, checkpoint, restore, steering, and contradiction injection are automated.
3. Measured outputs include epistemic-gain metrics, not just runtime health or survival.
4. Failure-shape evals exist for unresolved contradiction at stop time, partial completion under budget stop, stale advisory child output, and post-resume regress.
5. A governor can inspect one long run and see whether extra runtime purchased more justified work.
notes: Architectural judgment: long duration matters only if it buys more justified work, better caveats, cleaner unresolveds, or corrected claim posture under governance. Boundaries: do not treat more tokens, more cycles, or longer survival as gain; do not compare different recipes; do not hide lawful failure shapes because they look bad.
close_reason: The long-duration epistemic-gain harness now compares the same question in short vs long bounded live-corpus runs, proves one additional supported claim after compaction plus checkpoint/restore, and ships a lawful failure-shape family for unresolved contradiction, budget stop, post-resume steering regress, and stale advisory child output.

title: Red-team resume, steering, reconcile, and bounded-child merge semantics

Scope:

design: Primary refs: docs/design-docs/gpt54pro_full_closure_program_2026_04_13.md; docs/design-docs/alexandria_runtime_grounding.md; app/runtime/session.py. Architectural judgment: a plausible stale answer is worse than an obvious failure.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Transition red-team cases prove stale answer structures are invalidated or rebuilt when truth changes. 2. Invalidation traces and report/ledger diffs are emitted. 3. Cases cover resume-after-steer, reconcile-after-ground-patch, child merge under conflict, and post-resume drift.
notes: Closed on 2026-04-13 after the runtime learned to invalidate stale answer/report surfaces on delegated truth merges, the transition semantic red-team probe proved lawful invalidation or rebuild across resume-after-steer, ground-patch reconcile, child merge under conflict, and post-resume continuation, and durable artifacts landed under docs/generated/runtime/transition_semantic_red_team_probe_2026_04_13.
close_reason: Transition red-team probe implemented with resume, ground-patch, delegated-merge, and post-resume rebuild cases plus durable artifacts.

title: Harden runtime-owned answer-case semantics before reporting
description:
# Harden runtime-owned answer-case semantics before reporting

## Why this exists

The principal architect's direct read of the current report exposed that the
strongest readable artifact is better than the persisted runtime-owned
answer-case. Owned-source diagnosis in
`docs/design-docs/pre_live_runtime_synthesis_substrate_postmortem_2026_04_13.md`
shows that the active acceptance lane disabled provider synthesis, the local
fallback synthesis path is structurally thin, and the active reporting state
still persists the legacy `ReportPacket` compatibility shape while richer
semantics are reconstructed late during reporting.

## Scope

- make native answer/report packet state first-class in the active runtime path
- harden local answer-case synthesis so provider-off no longer degrades into
  one-claim-per-evidence output
- strengthen the pre-live quality gate so thin packet promotion cannot silently
  return
- require a fresh bounded reviewer read of the provider-off artifact as if they
  were deciding whether to contract Alexandria for bounded research-runtime
  work
- refresh the bounded acceptance/demo artifacts and claim notes honestly

## Non-goals

- prompt-only polish
- provider-only rescue
- new semantic authority layers
- comparison/domain expansion work

## Read first

- `docs/design-docs/pre_live_runtime_synthesis_substrate_postmortem_2026_04_13.md`
- `docs/exec-plans/active/pre_live_runtime_synthesis_hardening.md`
- `docs/design-docs/reporting_node_contract_2026_04_06.md`
- `app/runtime/session.py`
- `app/service/synthesis.py`
- `app/service/reporting.py`
- `app/types/reporting.py`

## First action

Add failing tests that prove the current hot path still persists downgraded
report-packet state, that provider-off answer construction on the live
acceptance lane is semantically thin, and that a fresh reviewer would still
hesitate to contract Alexandria on the basis of the current provider-off
artifact.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. The active runtime reporting path persists native answer/report packet semantics and keeps legacy `ReportPacket` downgrade as an adapter-only surface.
2. Provider-off on the contradiction-bearing live acceptance lane emits an answer-first grouped report with load-bearing caveats/unresolveds and no longer collapses into one-claim-per-evidence or OCR-fragment synthesis.
3. A fresh bounded reviewer can read the provider-off report as if judging whether to contract Alexandria for bounded research-runtime work and emit a pass / pass-with-conditions / decline decision with concrete reasons.
4. Pre-live validation fails on thin packet promotion, answer-support drift, contradiction suppression, or provider-off semantic weakness, and refreshed acceptance/demo artifacts plus claim notes remain coherent.

Architectural judgment: this is a runtime-owned answer-case hardening task, not a prompt-polish task.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The active runtime reporting path persists native answer/report packet semantics and keeps legacy ReportPacket downgrade as an adapter-only surface.
2. Provider-off on the contradiction-bearing live acceptance lane emits an answer-first grouped report with load-bearing caveats/unresolveds and no longer collapses into one-claim-per-evidence or OCR-fragment synthesis.
3. A fresh bounded reviewer can read the provider-off report as if judging whether to contract Alexandria for bounded research-runtime work and emit a pass / pass-with-conditions / decline decision with concrete reasons.
4. Pre-live validation fails on thin packet promotion, answer-support drift, contradiction suppression, or provider-off semantic weakness, and refreshed acceptance/demo artifacts plus claim notes remain coherent.

Release should treat this bead as pre-live blocking until the acceptance/demo artifacts and bounded reviewer decision prove the stronger provider-off posture.

title: Program 80 — Research Control Plane Rebuild
description:

Current reality:

Scope:

- restore pending tasks and task leases safely
- drain steering before work after resume
- revalidate readiness after resume and after ground change

title: Install a manifest-first context controller
description:

title: Upgrade bounded branches into autonomous research harnesses
description:
Why this exists:
Branches cannot remain parent-fed shells. Each live branch must be a bounded non-authoritative research harness that can plan, retrieve/tool, checkpoint, compact, resume, write workbench artifacts, and stop under contract.

Scope:
- move branch cognition into child runtimes where appropriate
- define tool/search/fetch permissions inside branch contracts
- allow bounded persistence without branch sovereignty
- externalize artifacts at boundary hops
- prove discard/re-entry without provider-memory dependence

Non-goals:
- no unconstrained agent spawning
- no provider session state as authority
- no prose-only branch returns

Read first:

- app/runtime/child_runtime.py
- app/runtime/session.py
- docs/generated/runtime/latest_program84_dossier_recursive_proof.json

Pre-mortem:
- Harness theater: child runtime gains fields while parent still does all research.
- Recursion runaway: child branches spawn or continue beyond contract.
- Provider-memory trap: continuation works only if the provider session survives.

Red TDD:
- A branch that returns prose without required workbench artifacts and result packet fails.
- A branch that resumes only from provider session state fails.
- A child harness that exceeds task budget or recurses without contract fails.

Green TDD:
- One long branch completes local task-shaped research, compacts/checkpoints, drops hot context, rehydrates from durable artifacts, and merges through the parent.
- Parent-owned truth and adjudication remain unchanged until merge/admission.

First action for claiming agent:
Map current parent-vs-child cognition split and identify exact operations to move into bounded child harnesses.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Child runtimes can perform bounded task-shaped research locally rather than acting as parent-fed shells. 2. Branches emit structured workbench products and merge proposals, not prose-only returns. 3. Branch hot context can be discarded and rehydrated from durable artifacts without provider-memory dependence. 4. Parent-owned truth and central adjudication remain enforced.

title: Install recursive adequacy and review loops
description:
Why this exists:
Recursive research needs doer/reviewer separation and stop/continue/reopen law. Review cannot be a final prose QA pass; it must reopen structured research through workbench and authority references.

Scope:
- define inner and outer adequacy verdicts
- bind review failures to task graph, workbench refs, authority refs, and merge objects
- emit ReviewVerdict and ReviewFailurePack
- reopen tasks structurally on failure

Non-goals:
- no reviewer truth ownership
- no silent text patching of unsupported claims
- no prose-only review object

Pre-mortem:
- Review theater: review identifies weaknesses but has no structural effect.
- Self-grading: workers approve their own adequacy.
- Report rescue: unsupported claims are edited down without re-entering research.

Red TDD:
- A review failure that does not change task-board/reopen state fails.
- Unsupported report claims fixed only by text edits fail.
- A worker self-approval with no parent/reviewer verdict fails.

Green TDD:
- One run shows report or synthesis, review failure, targeted task reopen, additional work, revised projection, and approval/downgrade.
- Review artifacts are machine-readable and reference exact workbench/authority refs.

First action for claiming agent:
Specify the minimum lawful ReviewVerdict and ReviewFailurePack surfaces needed to structurally reopen work before writing the loop.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Typed adequacy/review verdicts exist for branch-local and parent-level decisions. 2. Review failures bind to task graph, workbench refs, authority refs, and merge objects. 3. description:
Why this exists:
Retrospective review found the Codex-DR sandbox handoff was directionally right but too conceptual for autonomous build safety. The architect needed a concrete first boot path, root-gravity firewall, token-burn firewall, and required skill gates before benchmark or provider-backed execution.

Scope:
- add sandbox/codex-dr/docs/BOOTSTRAP_DOCTRINE.md
- update sandbox AGENTS, README, handoff memo, root plan, authority ledger, and ExecPlans
- make provider-off first boot the first execution lane
- make benchmark acquisition target calibration until bootstrap validation passes

Non-goals:
- no harness implementation
- no benchmark execution
- no provider-backed run

Read first:
- sandbox/codex-dr/docs/BOOTSTRAP_DOCTRINE.md
- sandbox/codex-dr/AGENTS.md
- docs/exec-plans/active/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff.md

First action for claiming agent:
Verify every sandbox entrypoint makes the bootstrap doctrine visible before benchmark or provider-backed execution.
acceptance_criteria:
1. sandbox/codex-dr/docs/BOOTSTRAP_DOCTRINE.md exists and defines autonomy gate, provider-off first boot, token-burn firewall, root-gravity firewall, skill gates, stop rules, and proof artifact.
2. sandbox AGENTS and README point to the bootstrap doctrine before benchmark execution.
3. The active Codex-DR ExecPlan and root plan make provider-off bootstrap the first execution lane.
4. Benchmark acquisition remains early target calibration, not execution authorization.
5. make check passes.
notes: PLAN_REQUIRED: docs/exec-plans/active/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff.md. Created from retrospective lessons learned after the initial handoff.
close_reason: Installed the Codex-DR bootstrap doctrine, updated sandbox/root authority surfaces, made provider-off bootstrap the first execution lane, added token and root-gravity firewalls, and gated benchmark execution.

title: Produce Codex-DR benchmark acquisition audit
description:
Why this exists:
The sandbox target depends on empirical benchmark reality, but benchmark work must not become the first execution lane. The architect must know which Grep-cited benchmarks can be acquired, run, scored, and compared before parity claims harden, after the bootstrap doctrine has locked the local-first path.

Scope:
- audit DRACO, DeepSearchQA, DeepResearch Bench, and Parcha-published benchmark material
- record source URLs, versions, licenses, access constraints, case counts, evaluator shapes, local reproduction steps, and scoring gaps
- classify claims as permitted, blocked, or requiring external evidence
- preserve the rule that benchmark acquisition calibrates the target and does not authorize benchmark execution

Non-goals:
- no harness implementation
- no parity claim
- no benchmark execution
- no provider-backed terminal-agent run
- no hand-selected score without manifest

Read first:
- sandbox/codex-dr/AGENTS.md
- sandbox/codex-dr/docs/BOOTSTRAP_DOCTRINE.md
- sandbox/codex-dr/docs/ARCHITECT_HANDOFF.md
- docs/references/grep_building_grep_deep_research_2026_03_16.md
- docs/design-docs/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff_2026_04_22.md

First action for claiming agent:
Confirm the bootstrap doctrine has been read and then create the acquisition table before downloading or generating any benchmark data.
acceptance_criteria:
1. sandbox/codex-dr/benchmark-manifests/benchmark_acquisition_audit.md exists.
2. Each benchmark family has source, version, access, license, case-count, evaluator, and local-run notes.
3. Claims are classified as permitted, blocked, or evidence-pending.
4. The audit states that benchmark execution waits for provider-off bootstrap validation and token manifest rules.
5. Any private, paid, or large raw data stays outside git.
6. The next parity-contract bead can cite the audit without chat context.
notes: PLAN_REQUIRED: docs/exec-plans/active/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff.md. First empirical target-calibration spine for the sandbox; execution remains gated by BOOTSTRAP_DOCTRINE.md.
close_reason: Completed Codex-DR benchmark acquisition audit with source/version/license/access/evaluator/local-run notes for DRACO, DeepSearchQA, DeepResearch Bench, and Parcha-published benchmark material; added token manifest template and preserved benchmark execution/provider-backed gates.

title: Write Codex-DR Grep parity contract
description:
Why this exists:
The sandbox needs a concrete parity contract so builders know what Grep-system-and-performance parity means before coding.

Scope:
- define system behaviors required for the sandbox
- bind benchmark obligations from the acquisition audit
- name proof artifacts, allowed claims, fake success, and non-claims
- distinguish benchmark-facing sandbox proof from later pharma service-runtime proof

Non-goals:
- no product service architecture
- no implementation code
- no benchmark scoring without acquired data

Read first:
- sandbox/codex-dr/docs/ARCHITECT_HANDOFF.md
- sandbox/codex-dr/benchmark-manifests/benchmark_acquisition_audit.md
- PLAN_TO_CREATE_ALEXANDRIA.md
- docs/references/grep_building_grep_deep_research_2026_03_16.md

First action for claiming agent:
Extract the required behaviors and proof gates from the benchmark audit and Grep references before drafting schemas.
acceptance_criteria:
1. sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/grep_parity_contract.md exists.
2. The contract names required cognitive behaviors, benchmark obligations, proof artifacts, allowed claims, blocked claims, and fake success.
3. The contract states the difference between sandbox parity proof and service-runtime readiness.
4. The next harness-contract bead can implement against the contract without redefining parity.
notes: PLAN_REQUIRED: docs/exec-plans/active/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff.md.
close_reason: Completed sandbox Grep parity contract with required cognitive behaviors, benchmark obligations from acquisition audit, proof artifacts, allowed/blocked claims, fake success conditions, and sandbox-vs-service-runtime boundary.

title: Specify Codex-DR terminal-agent harness contracts
description:
Why this exists:
Builders need explicit contracts for the sandbox machinery before they implement the CLI, terminal-agent boxes, run bundles, event mirrors, and QA loop.

Scope:
- specify CLI commands
- specify run bundle schema
- specify terminal-agent box config
- specify event mirror, CAS manifest, compaction receipt, branch return, review file, re-entry compiler, claim ledger, and benchmark score formats
- specify failure modes and validation hooks

Non-goals:
- no production service runtime design
- no hidden provider-state dependency
- no implementation code until contracts are reviewable

Read first:
- sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/grep_parity_contract.md
- sandbox/codex-dr/docs/ARCHITECT_HANDOFF.md
- docs/design-docs/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff_2026_04_22.md

First action for claiming agent:
List the required artifacts in one run bundle and assign each artifact an owner, schema shape, validator, and failure mode.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Harness specs exist under sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/.
2. CLI, run bundle, terminal-agent box, event mirror, CAS manifest, compaction receipt, branch return, review, re-entry, claim ledger, and benchmark score contracts are specified.
3. Each contract has validation hooks and failure modes.
4. The implementation bead can start without inventing file formats or control semantics.
notes: PLAN_REQUIRED: docs/exec-plans/active/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff.md.
close_reason: Completed Codex-DR harness contracts with CLI, run bundle, terminal-agent box, event envelope, artefact manifest/CAS, compaction, branch return, review, re-entry, claim ledger, benchmark placeholder, allowed-claims, validators, and failure modes.

title: Implement Codex-DR provider-off bootstrap harness
description:
Why this exists:
The sandbox must prove it can observe and validate its own research loop before any provider-backed terminal-agent run or benchmark execution. This bead implements the provider-off bootstrap lane required by BOOTSTRAP_DOCTRINE.md.

Scope:
- implement the local fixture bootstrap commands or equivalent scriptable control surface
- create deterministic fake planner, branch, reviewer, re-entry, report, compaction, claim-ledger, benchmark-score placeholder, and allowed-claims outputs
- implement validators that fail on missing required bundle pieces, missing causal linkage, missing claim custody, missing review re-entry, missing compaction receipt, or widened claims
- keep all generated run outputs under ignored sandbox run/tmp paths

Non-goals:
- no provider-backed/model-backed calls
- no benchmark execution
- no product service runtime implementation
- no broad root runtime rewrite

Read first:
- sandbox/codex-dr/docs/BOOTSTRAP_DOCTRINE.md
- sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/grep_parity_contract.md
- sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/ once harness contracts exist
- docs/exec-plans/active/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff.md

First action for claiming agent:
Write failing validator tests for the provider-off run bundle before implementing the bootstrap commands.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A provider-off local fixture run can be created without model/provider calls.
2. The run emits the bootstrap bundle required by BOOTSTRAP_DOCTRINE.md.
3. Validators fail on missing events, artefact manifest, claim ledger, review re-entry, compaction receipt, benchmark-score placeholder, or allowed-claims boundary.
4. Generated run outputs remain in ignored sandbox paths.
5. make check and the new bootstrap validation command pass.
6. The full proof-run bead remains blocked until this bead closes.
notes: PLAN_REQUIRED: create or extend an implementation ExecPlan before code changes. This bead is the first build step after contracts; it is deliberately provider-off.
close_reason: Implemented provider-off bootstrap harness with deterministic local fixture generation, semantic validators, red/green mutation tests, ignored run output, passing bootstrap validation command, ruff, pytest, git diff --check, and make check; no provider/model calls or benchmark execution.

title: Implement first full Codex-DR proof run
description:
Why this exists:
The sandbox must prove the full research loop, not only documentation or a small harness. This bead implements the first complete benchmark-facing run after contracts exist.

Scope:
- implement the alexandria-dr control surface
- implement run-bundle creation and validation
- run planner, branch research, recursive deepening, synthesis, review, re-entry, report, scoring, and allowed-claims output
- preserve event, artefact, compaction, and claim custody

Non-goals:
- no product service deployment
- no private customer data
- no unsupported parity claim beyond emitted proof

Read first:
- sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/grep_parity_contract.md
- sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/
- docs/exec-plans/active/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff.md

First action for claiming agent:
Write failing validation tests for the full run bundle before implementing the runner.
acceptance_criteria:
1. alexandria-dr commands exist in the chosen sandbox implementation location.
2. A complete proof run emits run_manifest.json, events.jsonl, artefact_manifest.json, plan.md, task_graph.json, adequacy criteria, branches, evidence, synthesis, contradictions, reviews, compactions, claim_ledger.json, report.md, benchmark_score.json, and allowed_claims.json.
3. Tests or validators fail on missing custody artifacts, missing review re-entry, missing benchmark score, or report claims without custody.
4. make check passes.
5. The final allowed-claims artifact matches the actual proof.
notes: Blocked after provider-off scaffolding: acceptance requires a complete benchmark-facing proof run with actual allowed execution and score/claim custody. Continuing would require explicit Principal approval for provider-backed terminal-agent/model execution or benchmark execution plus a run-specific token manifest. No provider/model calls or benchmark execution were run.

title: Probe boxed Codex runner capability
description:
Why this exists:
The corrected Codex-DR center is boxed Codex recursive research execution, not provider-off scaffolding. Before any benchmark smoke run, the sandbox must know whether a Codex/terminal-agent box can be launched locally with transcript capture and run-bundle custody.

Scope:
- inspect available local Codex/terminal-agent CLI surfaces
- run harmless help/version/dry-run probes only
- do not start provider-backed research or benchmark execution
- record commands attempted, launch availability, transcript-capture path, next command if available, and exact blocker if unavailable under sandbox/codex-dr/docs/ or harness-specs/

Pre-mortem:
- mistaking CLI presence for a proved boxed research run
- leaking secrets while inspecting config
- accidentally starting a provider/model call during probe
- failing to record the exact next command

Red/green TDD:
Red: boxed runner availability is unknown and no probe artifact exists.
Green: a repo-local probe artifact records command evidence, availability decision, transcript capture path, and next command/blocker without provider/model calls.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Probe artifact exists under sandbox/codex-dr/docs/ or sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/.
2. Artifact lists commands attempted and their outcomes.
3. Artifact states whether Codex box launch is available from this sandbox.
4. Artifact states transcript capture path available/unavailable.
5. Artifact records next command if available or exact blocker if unavailable.
6. No provider-backed research run or benchmark execution occurred.
close_reason: Completed boxed Codex runner capability probe: Codex CLI 0.120.0 and codex exec command surface are present, /usr/bin/script transcript capture works under ignored sandbox tmp path, no provider/model/benchmark run was executed, and result artifact is sandbox/codex-dr/docs/boxed_codex_runner_capability_probe_2026_04_22.md.

title: Create DRACO tiny smoke case manifest
description:
Why this exists:
A boxed smoke run needs a real benchmark-facing case without committing raw or large benchmark data. DRACO is the preferred first target because the acquisition audit classifies it as small, public, and rubric-shaped.

Scope:
- identify a tiny DRACO smoke case source pointer
- create a case manifest under sandbox/codex-dr/benchmark-manifests/ or harness-specs/
- keep raw/full dataset content out of git
- record license/access/source SHA facts from the acquisition audit or public metadata

Pre-mortem:
- committing raw benchmark data
- selecting a case without rubric/source pointer
- treating case acquisition as benchmark execution
- losing the 100-case DRACO context while using a one-case smoke

Red/green TDD:
Red: no smoke case manifest exists and the run cannot choose a case safely.
Green: a tiny manifest names exactly the smoke case pointer, source URL/version, allowed local cache path, and non-claims without raw/large data in git.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tiny DRACO smoke manifest exists in Codex-DR-owned scope.
2. Manifest cites DRACO source URL/version/license facts available from the acquisition audit or public metadata.
3. Manifest selects only the smoke subset, preferably one case.
4. Raw/full DRACO data is not committed.
5. Manifest states that case selection does not authorize benchmark execution or scores.
close_reason: Completed pointer-only DRACO tiny smoke manifest at sandbox/codex-dr/benchmark-manifests/draco_tiny_smoke_case_manifest.md: cites HF source, commit ce076749809027649ebd331bcb70f42bf720d387, MIT/public access, test.jsonl oid/size, selects row 0 only, keeps raw/full data out of git, and states no benchmark execution or score authorization.

title: Create run-specific token manifest for boxed smoke
description:
Why this exists:
Any provider/model-backed boxed smoke run needs explicit budget, stop rules, transcript policy, provider/tool policy, allowed claims, and non-claims before execution.

Scope:
- create a concrete run-specific token manifest for the DRACO smoke run
- cap budget and attempts for tiny smoke
- define transcript capture and secret/data policy
- state allowed claims and blocked claims
- do not execute model/provider calls as part of manifest creation

Pre-mortem:
- manifest is vague enough to allow uncapped spend
- transcript policy leaks secrets or stores private data
- allowed claims widen from smoke to parity
- benchmark score is implied without scorer bridge

Red/green TDD:
Red: provider-backed smoke cannot run because no bounded manifest exists.
Green: manifest is concrete enough that a runner can either execute within cap or fail closed before any spend.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Run-specific token manifest exists in Codex-DR-owned scope.
2. Manifest names bead, run id, purpose, runner, budget, stop rules, transcript path, data policy, provider/tool policy, allowed claims, and non-claims.
3. Manifest caps spend/attempts for a tiny smoke run.
4. Manifest blocks Grep parity, leaderboard, product readiness, and full benchmark claims.
5. No provider-backed call is made by this bead.
close_reason: Completed run-specific token manifest for DRACO boxed smoke at sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/draco_smoke_001_token_manifest.yaml with run id, purpose, runner, transcript path, data policy, provider/tool policy, budget caps, stop rules, allowed claims, and blocked parity/score/leaderboard/product claims. No provider-backed call was made.

title: Run boxed recursive research smoke
description:
Why this exists:
The first full proof-run lane must exercise a real boxed recursive research loop rather than provider-off fixtures. This bead runs the smallest allowed smoke once runner, case, and token manifest gates are satisfied.

Scope:
- launch boxed Codex/terminal-agent runner if probe permits
- capture transcript under ignored run/workspace paths
- emit planner, branch pointer/analysis/evidence, synthesis, review/fact-check, re-entry, report, events, artifact manifest, claim ledger, allowed claims, and benchmark placeholder or scorer output
- stop immediately on budget, secret, custody, or benchmark-access violation

Pre-mortem:
- help/version probe is mistaken for actual smoke execution
- runner starts without transcript capture
- branch output bypasses pointer/analysis/evidence contract
- review cannot cause re-entry
- run emits score or parity claim without scorer custody

Red/green TDD:
Red: no real boxed recursive smoke bundle exists.
Green: run bundle shows actual boxed execution or exact external blocker, with transcript/event/CAS custody and allowed claims bounded to the emitted proof.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Runner, case manifest, and token manifest gates are satisfied before execution.
2. Smoke run emits required recursive research artifacts or stops with exact blocker before spend.
3. Transcript capture is present for any boxed execution.
4. Review/fact-check pressure causes re-entry or records a justified no-reentry decision.
5. Allowed claims match actual emitted proof.
6. No unsupported benchmark score, Grep parity, leaderboard, or product-readiness claim is made.
notes: Preflight stopped before provider/model spend. Runner surface, transcript wrapper, DRACO smoke manifest, and run-specific token manifest exist, but codex exec help exposes no mechanical max-token or max-cost cap. Because sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/draco_smoke_001_token_manifest.yaml declares budget cap enforcement/monitoring a hard stop, boxed recursive smoke execution is blocked pending a CLI budget-cap mechanism or explicit Principal/main coordinator waiver for this one capped smoke run. See sandbox/codex-dr/docs/boxed_recursive_smoke_preflight_2026_04_22.md.

title: Bridge benchmark scoring for boxed smoke
description:
Why this exists:
Benchmark-facing smoke needs an explicit bridge from run outputs to DRACO/scorer expectations. Without that bridge, scores and comparisons remain blocked.

Scope:
- identify exact DRACO scorer/judge path for smoke output
- record whether scoring is local, external, model-judged, or blocked
- define score artifact schema and claim boundary
- do not run paid/model judging without token/scorer approval

Pre-mortem:
- treating report generation as benchmark scoring
- using public Grep/Parcha scores as Alexandria evidence
- running a judge without budget and prompt/version custody
- producing a numeric score without case/scorer manifest linkage

Red/green TDD:
Red: benchmark_score.json remains placeholder with no scorer bridge.
Green: bridge records exact scorer path or exact blocker, and any score-capable path has judge/scorer version, prompt, parameters, retry policy, and claim firewall.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Scoring bridge artifact exists in Codex-DR-owned scope.
2. Artifact cites DRACO scorer/judge path or exact blocker.
3. Artifact defines required scorer manifest fields.
4. Artifact states blocked/allowed claims after smoke.
5. No numeric score is emitted unless scorer execution is approved and custody-complete.

title: Write Codex-DR service-runtime bridge memo
description:
Why this exists:
After the first full sandbox proof run, Alexandria needs a clean boundary between what the terminal-agent sandbox proved and what the sellable service runtime still requires.

Scope:
- classify sandbox contracts as promotable, hardening-required, sandbox-only, or service-runtime replacement
- name missing product concerns: API, auth, tenancy, data policy, queues, durable workers, provider billing, source connector policy, secrets, audit export, and report surfaces
- preserve benchmark and proof-run lessons for product design

Non-goals:
- no product implementation
- no claim that the sandbox is production-ready
- no rewrite of the root charter

Read first:
- latest Codex-DR proof run bundle
- sandbox/codex-dr/harness-specs/grep_parity_contract.md
- docs/design-docs/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff_2026_04_22.md

First action for claiming agent:
Inventory every sandbox contract and classify its service-runtime fate before recommending product architecture.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A service-runtime bridge memo exists under docs/design-docs/ or sandbox/codex-dr/docs/.
2. Every major sandbox contract is classified as promotable, hardening-required, sandbox-only, or service-runtime replacement.
3. Product-runtime missing surfaces are named explicitly.
4. Claims separate benchmark-facing sandbox proof from client-deployable service readiness.
notes: PLAN_REQUIRED: docs/exec-plans/active/codex_dr_sandbox_architect_handoff.md.

title: Resolve missing Context Pack 07
description:
## Purpose
Close the known Context Pack intake gap before design work claims complete Context Pack grounding.

## Scope
Obtain Context Pack 07 from Linear or the Principal, import it under `docs/references/third_party_reimagining/`, update the intake compression and registry, or record an explicit Principal waiver that design work may proceed without it.

## Read First
- `docs/references/third_party_reimagining/2026_04_20_context_pack_00_master_index.md`
- `docs/design-docs/alexandria_reimagining_context_pack_intake_2026_04_20.md`
- `docs/design-docs/third_party_reimagining_artifact_registry_2026_04_20.md`
- `docs/exec-plans/active/reimagined_alexandria_authority_migration.md`

## Non-Goals
Do not infer Pack 07 contents from old repo material unless the Principal explicitly waives the missing source and assigns repo audit as the substitute.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Either Pack 07 is imported, indexed, and summarized with authority status, or a dated Principal waiver is recorded repo-locally.
- The intake document no longer leaves the Pack 07 status ambiguous.
- Any follow-up old-spec-material audit implication is captured in the legacy audit bead or a new bead.

## First Action
Search Linear or ask the Principal for Pack 07, then record the outcome in repo-local docs.
acceptance_criteria: Either Pack 07 is imported, indexed, and summarized with authority status, or a dated Principal waiver is recorded repo-locally. The intake document no longer leaves the Pack 07 status ambiguous.
notes: Blocks full Commission Restatement confidence and old-spec-material reconciliation.

title: Update authority maps after root plan ratification
description:
## Purpose
Make the repo entrypoints tell future agents the new authority story after `PLAN_TO_CREATE_ALEXANDRIA.md` is ratified.

## Scope
Update `AGENTS.md`, `canon/CANON.md`, `docs/README.md`, `docs/GOLDEN_RULES.md`, and the third-party artifact registry so the root plan is read before legacy numbered NLSpecs for reimagining work. Record that old NLSpecs are pending audit rather than automatically binding.

## Read First
- `PLAN_TO_CREATE_ALEXANDRIA.md`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `canon/CANON.md`
- `docs/design-docs/third_party_reimagining_artifact_registry_2026_04_20.md`
- `docs/WORKFLOW.md`
- `docs/exec-plans/active/reimagined_alexandria_authority_migration.md`

## Non-Goals
- Do not delete old specs.
- Do not rewrite runtime code.
- Do not claim implementation parity.
- Do not pre-judge individual spec retention.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Repo entrypoints point to `PLAN_TO_CREATE_ALEXANDRIA.md` as the primary reimagining plan after ratification.
- Old numbered NLSpecs are explicitly marked for audit before reuse.
- `make check` passes or any failure is documented with a follow-up bead.

## First Action
Confirm the Principal has ratified the root plan, then update the entrypoint docs.
acceptance_criteria: Repo entrypoints point to PLAN_TO_CREATE_ALEXANDRIA as the primary reimagining plan after ratification; old numbered NLSpecs are explicitly marked for audit before reuse; make check passes or any failure is documented with a follow-up bead.
notes: Depends on root plan ratification. May be done before the full legacy audit, but must not pre-judge individual spec retention.

title: Audit legacy NLSpecs and repo assets against the reimagined plan
description:
## Purpose
Classify existing numbered specs and current repo assets so the reimagined Alexandria can reuse value without inheriting drift.

## Scope
Audit `canon/specs/`, active design docs, Program 80-90 proof surfaces, data pipeline material, and current runtime code against `PLAN_TO_CREATE_ALEXANDRIA.md`. Classify each relevant source as retained, superseded, donor material, substrate, quarantine, or requires rewrite.

## Read First
- `PLAN_TO_CREATE_ALEXANDRIA.md`
- `canon/CANON.md`
- `canon/specs/`
- `docs/SYSTEM_MAP.md`
- `docs/KNOWLEDGE_MAP.md`
- `docs/design-docs/alexandria_reimagining_context_pack_intake_2026_04_20.md`
- `docs/generated/runtime/latest_allowed_claims_registry.json`
- `docs/exec-plans/active/reimagined_alexandria_authority_migration.md`

## Non-Goals
- Do not rewrite specs or code inside this bead unless the audit itself needs a tiny index update.
- Do not let current proof receipts widen the new plan by inertia.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A repo-local audit artifact exists and classifies legacy specs and major repo surfaces.
- Retained material has rationale.
- Superseded or quarantined material has a non-use rule.
- Unresolved items become follow-up beads.
- The audit cites the root plan and Context Pack status.

## First Action
Open the ExecPlan, then inventory `canon/specs/` and the current proof surfaces before assigning classifications.
acceptance_criteria: A repo-local audit artifact exists and classifies legacy specs and major repo surfaces; retained material has rationale; superseded/quarantined material has a non-use rule; unresolved items become follow-up beads; the audit cites the root plan and Context Pack status.
notes: PLAN_REQUIRED. Use docs/exec-plans/active/reimagined_alexandria_authority_migration.md before deep audit work. Output should be a repo-local audit artifact classifying legacy specs and repo surfaces.

title: Produce Commission Restatement Candidate
description:
## Purpose
Satisfy the Commissioning Brief clarification turn before Alexandria Design Document work begins.

## Scope
Write a concise Commission Restatement that summarizes Alexandria telos, commercial wedge, binding invariants, design-doc quality bar, ambiguity points, and proposed readings. Incorporate the ratified root plan and the resolved Pack 07 status.

## Read First
- `PLAN_TO_CREATE_ALEXANDRIA.md`
- `canon/specs/ALEXANDRIA_CHARTER.md`
- `docs/references/third_party_reimagining/2026_04_20_alexandria_design_document_commissioning_brief_v1.md`
- `docs/design-docs/alexandria_reimagining_context_pack_intake_2026_04_20.md`
- `docs/exec-plans/active/reimagined_alexandria_authority_migration.md`

## Non-Goals
- Do not start subsystem design.
- Do not write event schemas, WBS, or implementation code.
- Do not silently resolve Charter-level questions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A repo-local Commission Restatement Candidate exists.
- It names ambiguities and proposed readings.
- It is ready for Principal ratification or correction.
- It does not begin the Design Document deliverable.

## First Action
Read the Commissioning Brief and draft the one-page restatement against the current ratified/waived source set.
acceptance_criteria: A repo-local Commission Restatement Candidate exists, names ambiguities and proposed readings, and is ready for Principal ratification or correction. It does not silently resolve Charter-level questions.
notes: Depends on ratified/settled root plan and Pack 07 resolution or waiver.

title: Open the commissioned Alexandria Design Document program
description:
## Purpose
Enter the Commissioning Brief design-document phase only after authority, legacy audit, and Commission Restatement gates are ready.

## Scope
Create the document-production plan for the required Design Document set: master index, per-subsystem docs, event log schema, Anthropic tool specs, artefact formats, control-flow spec, 80-120 bead WBS, divergence/gap resolution, out-of-scope section, and interface-stability commitment.

## Read First
- `PLAN_TO_CREATE_ALEXANDRIA.md`
- the ratified Commission Restatement
- `docs/references/third_party_reimagining/2026_04_20_alexandria_design_document_commissioning_brief_v1.md`
- `docs/references/third_party_reimagining/2026_04_20_context_pack_05_divergence_flags.md`
- `docs/references/third_party_reimagining/2026_04_20_context_pack_06_inference_gaps.md`
- the legacy audit artifact from `alexandriacleanroom-91.4`
- `docs/exec-plans/active/reimagined_alexandria_authority_migration.md`

## Non-Goals
